prd.oneLiner: An app where employees find and book a free meeting room in three taps and see live availability.
prd.goal: Within 6 months of launch, move 80% of internal room bookings into the app and cut the no-show rate below 5%.
prd.whyNow: Hybrid work concentrates demand for meeting rooms, yet bookings still happen over chat or word of mouth — double bookings and no-shows are rising, and the need for space-utilization data keeps growing.
prd.problem: Booking status is scattered, so finding a free room is hard (the employee's job: grab an empty room right now), and double bookings and no-shows keep recurring.
prd.solution: Fast booking on top of live availability, confirmation and reminder notifications, and a usage-analytics dashboard for admins.
prd.alternatives: Today it's messenger announcements, paper sign-up sheets, and general-purpose calendars (Google Calendar) — none offer live availability or no-show management.
prd.differentiator: Combines company SSO accounts with room availability to support instant booking of free rooms and automatic no-show tracking.

targets.name: Employee A
targets.needs: Grab a free meeting room quickly, right now
targets.pain: Status is scattered, so finding an empty room is a chore

targets.name: Team lead
targets.needs: Share time and place with attendees in one step
targets.pain: Verbal or chat announcements cause people to be missed

targets.name: Facilities admin
targets.needs: Manage utilization and no-shows with data
targets.pain: No data to base operational decisions on

scenarios.text: Employee A checks free rooms from Home for a 2 p.m. meeting, picks a 3rd-floor room, adds four attendees, and confirms the booking. A confirmation notification goes out to the attendees.
Admin B reviews per-room utilization and no-show rates on the weekly dashboard and adjusts the operating hours of under-used rooms.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and exception handling status.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and exception handling status.

kpis.name: Average taps to complete a booking
kpis.method: Average taps to the completion event from logs

assumptions: Every employee has a company SSO account
Facilities provides the room list and capacity data
No-shows can be detected as booked-but-not-checked-in
risks: Uncertain schedule for company SSO integration
Accuracy of availability in rooms without sensors
Low statistical confidence early on due to sparse data
openQuestions: Should there be a no-show penalty policy?
constraints: Must work inside the corporate network (VPN)
Personal data may not be stored externally per company policy
iOS, Android, and web must be supported simultaneously
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
